humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers. Fetched-only rows replay through ingest, exported durable writes require active lease context, and DB tests cover crash-after-fetch-before-ingest plus missing-worker write bypass. External fact-table idempotency and in-memory transaction fidelity remain follow-ups.

findings.title: Source importer fact idempotency remains adapter-owned
findings.description: The generic crawler cannot detect arbitrary external fact-table writes if a source-specific ingestStep writes facts and crashes before commitStepImport. The repair correctly treats this as an importer contract, with tests modeling idempotent upserts.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit found the generic crawler has no source-specific knowledge of external fact tables; adapter importers must provide idempotent writes or durable import markers.

findings.impact: Future source adapters could duplicate facts if they do not implement upsert/idempotency behavior around crawler step commits.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Source adapter contract requires idempotent fact writes or source-specific durable import markers.
VNDB, EGS, DLsite, Steam, IGDB, and Wikidata adapter specs depend on the idempotency contract.

proposedDagNode.title: Source adapter idempotent fact import contract
proposedDagNode.summary: Define and test the source-adapter contract for idempotent catalog fact writes around crawler commitStepImport so future source-specific importers cannot duplicate facts after crashes.
deliverables: Adapter spec dependency notes for VNDB, EGS, DLsite, Steam, IGDB, and Wikidata
acceptanceCriteria: Adapter imports must use upserts or durable import markers before they can be marked production-ready.
A crash after fact writes but before crawler step commit can be replayed without duplicating facts.
Future source adapter DAG nodes cite this contract in acceptance criteria.

findings.title: In-memory crawler repository is not transactionally equivalent
findings.description: The in-memory test repository matches lease and fetched/imported semantics, but commitStepImport still updates step state before saving rate limit and checkpoint. It does not provide DB-equivalent transaction rollback.

evidence.detail: Final re-audit classified this as test-double fidelity rather than an acceptance blocker because DB-backed repository tests cover the durable semantics.

findings.impact: App/in-memory tests can miss ordering bugs that only DB-backed transaction tests catch.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: In-memory commitStepImport either mirrors DB atomic behavior or is documented as non-transactional.
App tests that rely on transaction semantics use a DB-backed repository or an explicit failure-injection test double.
Failure injection covers rate-limit/checkpoint persistence errors.

proposedDagNode.title: Crawler in-memory repository transaction fidelity
proposedDagNode.summary: Align the in-memory catalog crawler repository with DB commitStepImport transaction semantics or make its non-transactional test-double boundary explicit with failure-injection coverage.
deliverables: Transaction-equivalent in-memory commitStepImport or documented limitation
Failure-injection tests for rate-limit and checkpoint persistence errors
App test guidance for DB-backed crawler semantics
acceptanceCriteria: In-memory tests cannot falsely pass when DB transaction ordering would fail.
Failure injection covers imported marker, rate-limit, and checkpoint ordering.
Documentation states when DB-backed tests are required.
